sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for NES flight combat game

special_mechanics.name: Aircraft Flight Control
special_mechanics.description: Player controls F-14 Tomcat fighter jet from cockpit point of view. Game focuses on flight dynamics rather than platformer physics. Speed and angle control are critical mechanics, especially during landing sequences.

special_mechanics.name: Landing Sequence
special_mechanics.description: Final landing phase uses side-view perspective of aircraft carrier approach. Requires precise speed and angle control. Documented as particularly difficult mechanic.

special_mechanics.name: Missile Selection and Targeting
special_mechanics.description: Three missile types available with varying target locking areas. VS. System version gives 10 seconds to select, NES version gives unlimited time.

special_mechanics.name: Fuel Management
special_mechanics.description: Refueling mechanic with time limits in VS. System version (25 seconds). NES version allows unlimited refueling time. Low fuel leads to life loss.

tier_notes: Tier 3 data: Top Gun is a flight combat simulator without traditional platformer physics. Available documentation focuses on gameplay mechanics, level design, and regional differences rather than detailed movement physics parameters. No sub-pixel precision, gravity values, or velocity measurements are documented in available sources. The game emphasizes cockpit-view dogfighting and landing mechanics rather than character movement physics.
